title: MEOS Energy & Carbon Management Module — User Training Video

scenes.title: MEOS Energy & Carbon Management Module
scenes.subtitle: User Training Video
scenes.meta: Keppel South Central  ·  Janusd Global Delivery
scenes.narration: Welcome to the MEOS Energy and Carbon Management Module user training video. This video covers 8 chapters and 22 key functional screens, providing a comprehensive guide to monitoring energy consumption, managing budgets, and optimizing energy efficiency.

scenes.title: Report List Overview
bullets: All historical reports listed chronologically
System auto-generates weekly and monthly reports
Filter by type, date range, or keyword
scenes.narration: Open the Energy Review Reports page to see all historical reports sorted by date. The system automatically generates weekly and monthly reports. Use the filter bar to narrow results by report type, date range, or keywords. Click the action buttons in each row to view details or trigger an update.

scenes.title: Period Filter & Search
bullets: Filter by All / Weekly / Monthly report types
Free date range selection for custom periods
Keyword search supports fuzzy matching
scenes.narration: Expand the filter panel to select report types: All, Weekly, or Monthly. Set a custom date range to locate reports from any period. The keyword search field supports fuzzy matching, so you can quickly find the target report without entering the exact title.

scenes.title: Report Actions & Pagination
bullets: Detail button — view full report content
Update button — re-generate with latest data
Pagination controls at the bottom of the list
scenes.narration: Each report row provides two action buttons. Click Detail to open the full report, or click Update to trigger a re-generation that pulls the latest energy data. The pagination control at the bottom lets you browse through large report archives efficiently.

scenes.title: Budget Overview Dashboard
bullets: Top cards: actual energy vs. control target
YoY and MoM trend indicators displayed
Instant visibility into monthly energy status
scenes.narration: The Energy Budget Monitoring page presents the current month's budget execution at a glance. The top summary cards compare actual energy consumption against the control target, and display both year-over-year and month-over-month change indicators to help you quickly assess whether energy usage is on track.

scenes.title: Annual Overview Chart
bullets: Full-year cumulative actual energy with time-progress baseline
Daily actual vs. plan comparison by month
Identify seasonal peaks and anomalous periods
scenes.narration: The annual overview chart shows cumulative energy consumption for the full year alongside a time-progress reference line. Below it, a month-by-month breakdown compares daily actual usage against the plan, making it easy to identify seasonal peaks, high-consumption periods, and opportunities for optimization throughout the year.

scenes.title: Sub-Item Energy Table
bullets: Energy breakdown by type: HVAC, Lighting, and more
Click column headers to sort and compare
Budget settings entry for parameter configuration
scenes.narration: The sub-item energy table breaks total consumption down by category — HVAC, lighting, and other systems — providing a side-by-side comparison against budget targets. Click any column header to sort the data. The Budget Settings link in the table takes you directly to the parameter configuration page.

scenes.title: Monthly Cost vs. Budget
bullets: Actual total cost vs. budget target for the month
Export detailed report from the top-right corner
Switch months using the period selector
scenes.narration: The Cost Budget Analysis page compares the month's actual total expenditure against the approved budget. Use the export button in the top-right corner to download a detailed breakdown report. The month selector at the top lets you switch between any historical period for retrospective review.

scenes.title: Single-Energy Cost Detail
bullets: Cost breakdown by energy type: electricity, water, gas, heat
Actual usage and incurred cost per energy type
Monthly average temperature shown for context
scenes.narration: The single-energy cost detail section separates expenditure by energy type — electricity, water, gas, and heat — showing both actual consumption volume and the corresponding cost for each. The monthly average temperature is also displayed as a contextual reference for energy analysis and anomaly investigation.

scenes.title: Annual Budget Execution Summary
bullets: Month-by-month bar chart: actual cost vs. budget
Cumulative full-year cost totals
Trend line for monthly cost trajectory
scenes.narration: The annual budget execution summary uses a month-by-month bar chart to compare actual costs against budget targets throughout the year. The cumulative cost totals below provide a full-year view, while the trend line helps management evaluate spending trajectory and forecast whether the annual budget will be met.

scenes.title: Treemap Heat Map Overview
bullets: Hierarchical treemap of building energy distribution
Block size proportional to energy consumption
Red highlights indicate significant deviations
scenes.narration: The Energy Usage Map visualizes building energy distribution as a hierarchical treemap. Block size is proportional to energy consumption, so the largest energy consumers are immediately visible. Blocks flagged with significant deviations are highlighted in red, allowing operations staff to pinpoint high-consumption units at a glance.

scenes.title: Drill-Down View
bullets: Click any block's Drill Down button to expand
View sub-item distribution within each node
Example: Chiller, pump, fan breakdown in HVAC
scenes.narration: Click the Drill Down button on any block to expand that node and reveal its sub-item energy distribution. For example, drilling into the HVAC system shows the individual contributions of chillers, pumps, fans, and other components — enabling granular analysis of where energy is consumed within each system.

scenes.title: Legend & Display Controls
bullets: Legend explains positive-deviation and focus-area colors
Top controls set the number of display hierarchy levels
Save current view as the default configuration
scenes.narration: The legend panel on the right explains the color coding: orange marks positive deviations, and red marks focus areas requiring immediate attention. The display level controls at the top let you adjust how many hierarchy levels the map renders. After customizing the view, click Save to persist it as the default layout for all users.

scenes.title: Analysis vs. Comparison Modes
bullets: Analysis mode: deep-dive into a single period
Comparison mode: side-by-side multi-period view
Select energy model and time range as query inputs
scenes.narration: The Energy Consumption Query page offers two modes. Analysis mode enables deep investigation of a single time period, while Comparison mode displays multiple periods side by side for trend and variance analysis. Choose the appropriate energy calculation model and define your time range before running the query.

scenes.title: Query Panel & Node Selection
bullets: Define time interval and energy calculation model
Select specific sub-items or load nodes to analyze
Click Query to load results into the dashboard
scenes.narration: In the query conditions panel, set the analysis time interval and choose the energy calculation model. The node list below lets you select specific sub-items or load nodes as the analysis targets. Once your parameters are configured, click Query and the results will load directly into the dashboard charts.

scenes.title: Reference Information Panel
bullets: Total energy for the selected period
Daily month-over-month change rate
Daily year-over-year change rate
scenes.narration: The reference information panel highlights three key performance indicators for the selected period: total energy consumption, the daily month-over-month change rate, and the daily year-over-year change rate. These three figures are the primary signals for determining whether current energy levels are within normal operating range.

scenes.title: Sankey Diagram Overview
bullets: Sankey chart maps the full building energy chain
Flow width proportional to energy volume
Transmission loss rates annotated at each node
scenes.narration: The Energy Flow Diagram uses a Sankey chart to visualize the entire energy chain within the building. Flow line width is proportional to energy volume, making the highest-consumption pathways immediately apparent. Transmission loss rates are annotated at each node, helping facilities teams identify where energy is being lost in the distribution network.

scenes.title: Energy Flow Model Sidebar
bullets: Left sidebar lists all available energy flow models
Click any model to switch analysis perspective
Create and save custom models for different scenarios
scenes.narration: The left sidebar lists all available energy flow models. Click any model to instantly switch the diagram to that analytical perspective. The system ships with several built-in templates, and you can create and save additional custom models to accommodate different reporting scenarios or stakeholder requirements.

scenes.title: Period Filter & Legend
bullets: Switch between monthly, quarterly, and yearly views
Select any historical month for past data
Legend identifies electricity, gas, and other energy types
scenes.narration: The top filter bar lets you toggle between monthly, quarterly, and yearly statistical periods. You can also select any specific historical month to review past energy flow data. The legend area identifies the color coding for different energy carriers — electricity, gas, and other types — displayed in the Sankey flows.

scenes.title: Allocation Filters & Export
bullets: Filter by month and energy type
Toggle between consumption volume and cost views
One-click Excel export for downstream reporting
scenes.narration: The Energy Allocation Report supports filtering by month and energy type. Switch the data view between consumption volume and cost using the toggle control. All filtered results can be exported to Excel in one click, providing the data source needed for internal cost recovery and financial reporting workflows.

scenes.title: Departmental Allocation Table
bullets: Breakdown by department: usage and cost per unit
Precise data basis for internal cost chargebacks
Exported report ready for financial reconciliation
scenes.narration: The allocation table lists each department's energy consumption and corresponding cost, providing the authoritative data basis for internal cost chargebacks. The exported report can be fed directly into cost-splitting calculations and financial statements, enabling full energy cost accountability across all departments.

scenes.title: Diagnosis Report Overview
bullets: System diagnostics on equipment operating conditions
Weekly issue summary with frequency and impact scope
Drill into individual issues for detailed analysis
scenes.narration: The Operational Diagnostics Report performs a systematic assessment of equipment operating conditions and generates a structured issue summary on a weekly basis. The report lists each detected anomaly category with its frequency and affected scope, allowing facilities managers to prioritize remediation by impact and urgency.

scenes.title: Abnormality Analysis & Recommendations
bullets: Event background and root cause analysis per anomaly
Specific improvement recommendations provided
Mark each recommendation with handling progress
scenes.narration: The abnormality analysis page describes the context of each detected event, delivers a root cause conclusion, and provides targeted improvement recommendations. Every recommendation can be tagged with its handling progress status, ensuring that identified issues are tracked through to resolution and that findings translate into measurable operational improvements.

scenes.title: MEOS Energy & Carbon Management Module
scenes.subtitle: Thank you for watching
scenes.meta: For support, please contact Janusd Global Delivery
scenes.narration: Thank you for watching the MEOS Energy and Carbon Management Module user training video. For technical questions or operational support, please reach out to the Janusd Global Delivery team. We wish you confident and effective use of the system.
